task_completion.description: Import the USLCI database and LCIA methods into OpenLCA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenLCA Navigation pane shows the imported USLCI database and LCIA methods (e.g., TRACI 2.1) under the respective folders, or an import success message is visible.

task_completion.description: Create at least two product systems: one for a combination truck and one for rail/train.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Product systems' folder in the OpenLCA Navigation pane is expanded and shows at least two systems (one referencing truck/road and one referencing rail/train), or their respective tabs are open in the editor.

task_completion.description: Create an OpenLCA Project, add the product systems as variants, and select TRACI 2.1 as the LCIA method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Project setup tab is open in OpenLCA. The 'Compared variants' section lists the truck and rail product systems, and the 'LCIA method' field is set to TRACI 2.1.

task_completion.description: Run the project comparison to generate results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Project Report tab is open in OpenLCA, displaying comparison charts or tables (e.g., relative indicator results) for the truck and rail variants.

task_completion.description: Export the comparison results to the specified CSV file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog saving to '~/LCA_Results/transport_project_comparison.csv', or a terminal/file manager/text editor showing the existence and contents of this CSV file.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenLCA GUI to create the project and run the comparison.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenLCA application interface being actively used (e.g., clicking through menus, setting up the project), rather than just running a script to generate the output.

integrity.description: The exported CSV contains actual LCA comparison data.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV contents are visible, they should contain numerical data, impact categories (like Global warming), and units (like kg CO2 eq), not just dummy text with the required keywords.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenLCA application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenLCA window is visible with its characteristic layout (Navigation pane on the left, editor in the center), confirming the software was actually launched and used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The USLCI database is located at /home/ga/LCA_Imports/uslci_database.zip and LCIA methods at /home/ga/LCA_Imports/lcia_methods.zip. The task requires creating at least 2 product systems and 1 project. The output must be saved to /home/ga/LCA_Results/transport_project_comparison.csv. The exported results should contain keywords related to truck (truck/lorry/road), rail (rail/train/locomotive), and global warming (global warming/gwp/climate/co2). TRACI 2.1 is the required LCIA method, which includes the 'Global warming' impact category.
